chain.name: Saint Peter
chain.connection: Called directly by Jesus Christ

chain.name: Saint Paul
chain.connection: Called directly by the risen Christ on the road to Damascus (Acts 9)

chain.name: Saint Clement of Rome
chain.connection: Ordained by Saint Peter

chain.name: Saint Peter
chain.connection: Called directly by Jesus Christ

chain.name: Didache
chain.connection: Compiled from the oral teaching of the Twelve Apostles

chain.name: The Twelve Apostles
chain.connection: Called directly by Jesus Christ

chain.name: Saint Ignatius of Antioch
chain.connection: Disciple of the Apostle John; tradition holds he was also taught by Saint Peter, who appointed him Bishop of Antioch

chain.name: Saint John the Apostle
chain.connection: Called directly by Jesus Christ

chain.name: Saint Polycarp
chain.connection: Disciple of the Apostle John, who appointed him Bishop of Smyrna

chain.name: Saint John the Apostle
chain.connection: Called directly by Jesus Christ

chain.name: Saint Irenaeus of Lyon
chain.connection: Disciple of Saint Polycarp as a young man in Smyrna; wrote: 'I listened to his words carefully and stored them in my memory'

chain.name: Saint Polycarp
chain.connection: Disciple of the Apostle John

chain.name: Saint John the Apostle
chain.connection: Called directly by Jesus Christ

chain.name: Saint Justin Martyr
chain.connection: Converted through encounter with an old man who knew the apostles' teaching; studied in the churches of Rome and Ephesus which were founded by the apostles

chain.name: Apostolic Churches of Rome & Ephesus
chain.role: Churches founded by Peter, Paul, and John
chain.connection: Founded by the Apostles Peter, Paul, and John

chain.name: Tertullian
chain.connection: Trained in the faith of the Church of Carthage and Rome; appealed to apostolic churches as the standard of truth

chain.name: Church of Rome
chain.role: Founded by Saints Peter and Paul
chain.connection: Founded by the Apostles Peter and Paul

chain.name: Saint Clement of Alexandria
chain.role: Head of the Catechetical School of Alexandria
chain.connection: Studied under Pantaenus, head of the Alexandrian school

chain.name: Pantaenus
chain.role: Founder/Head of the Catechetical School of Alexandria
chain.connection: Tradition records he was taught by men who had known the apostles; sent as missionary to India

chain.name: Apostolic Tradition of Alexandria
chain.connection: Saint Mark the Evangelist, companion of Saint Peter

chain.name: Origen
chain.role: Theologian, Head of the Catechetical School of Alexandria
chain.connection: Pupil of Saint Clement of Alexandria

chain.name: Saint Clement of Alexandria
chain.role: Head of the Catechetical School of Alexandria
chain.connection: Studied under Pantaenus

chain.name: Pantaenus
chain.connection: Taught by men who knew the apostles

chain.name: Apostolic Tradition of Alexandria
chain.connection: Saint Mark the Evangelist, companion of Saint Peter

chain.name: Saint Cyprian of Carthage
chain.connection: Converted and mentored by the priest Caecilius; deeply influenced by Tertullian, whom he called 'The Master'

chain.name: Tertullian
chain.connection: Trained in the apostolic faith of Rome and Carthage

chain.name: Church of Rome
chain.role: Founded by Saints Peter and Paul
chain.connection: Founded by the Apostles Peter and Paul

chain.name: Saint Athanasius of Alexandria
chain.role: Bishop of Alexandria, Doctor of the Church
chain.connection: Secretary to Bishop Alexander of Alexandria; ordained by Alexander and succeeded him

chain.name: Alexander of Alexandria
chain.connection: Bishop in the apostolic see of Alexandria, in unbroken succession from Saint Mark

chain.name: Apostolic See of Alexandria
chain.connection: Saint Mark the Evangelist, companion of Saint Peter

chain.name: Saint Basil the Great
chain.role: Bishop of Caesarea, Doctor of the Church
chain.connection: Grandson of Saint Macrina the Elder, who was taught by Saint Gregory Thaumaturgus

chain.name: Saint Macrina the Elder
chain.connection: Disciple of Saint Gregory Thaumaturgus

chain.name: Saint Gregory Thaumaturgus
chain.connection: Pupil of Origen

chain.name: Origen
chain.role: Head of the Catechetical School of Alexandria
chain.connection: Pupil of Clement of Alexandria, in the Alexandrian apostolic tradition

chain.name: Saint Ambrose of Milan
chain.role: Bishop of Milan, Doctor of the Church
chain.connection: Elected Bishop of Milan by the people; trained in the Roman apostolic tradition

chain.name: Apostolic See of Rome
chain.role: The church Ambrose looked to as the standard of faith
chain.connection: Founded by the Apostles Peter and Paul

chain.name: Saint John Chrysostom
chain.role: Archbishop of Constantinople, Doctor of the Church
chain.connection: Studied under Diodore of Tarsus at the school of Antioch

chain.name: Diodore of Tarsus
chain.role: Bishop of Tarsus, Head of the School of Antioch
chain.connection: Trained in the Antiochene tradition, the apostolic see founded by Saint Peter

chain.name: Apostolic See of Antioch
chain.role: Church where believers were first called Christians (Acts 11:26)
chain.connection: Founded by the Apostle Peter, who served as its first bishop

chain.name: Saint Augustine of Hippo
chain.role: Bishop of Hippo, Doctor of the Church
chain.connection: Baptized and mentored by Saint Ambrose of Milan

chain.name: Saint Ambrose of Milan
chain.role: Bishop of Milan, Doctor of the Church
chain.connection: Bishop in communion with the apostolic See of Rome

chain.name: Apostolic See of Rome
chain.role: Founded by Saints Peter and Paul
chain.connection: Founded by the Apostles Peter and Paul

chain.name: Saint Cyril of Jerusalem
chain.role: Bishop of Jerusalem, Doctor of the Church
chain.connection: Bishop of Jerusalem, the mother church of all Christianity

chain.name: Apostolic See of Jerusalem
chain.role: The original church, led first by Saint James the Just
chain.connection: Saint James the Just, 'brother of the Lord,' first Bishop of Jerusalem, appointed by the apostles

chain.name: Saint Cyril of Alexandria
chain.role: Patriarch of Alexandria, Doctor of the Church
chain.connection: Nephew and successor of Patriarch Theophilus of Alexandria; bishop in the apostolic see of Saint Mark

chain.name: Apostolic See of Alexandria
chain.connection: Saint Mark the Evangelist, companion of Saint Peter

chain.name: Saint Ephrem the Syrian
chain.role: Deacon, Doctor of the Church, Hymnographer
chain.connection: Studied under Saint Jacob of Nisibis, who attended the Council of Nicaea

chain.name: Saint Jacob of Nisibis
chain.connection: Bishop in the Syriac apostolic tradition, attendee of the First Council of Nicaea (325)

chain.name: Syriac Apostolic Tradition
chain.role: Churches evangelized by the Apostle Thomas and Addai (Thaddeus)
chain.connection: Tradition traces to the Apostle Thomas and Addai, one of the Seventy Disciples

chain.name: Saint Jerome
chain.role: Priest, Doctor of the Church, Translator of the Vulgate
chain.connection: Studied under Saint Gregory Nazianzen in Constantinople; ordained in Antioch

chain.name: Saint Gregory Nazianzen
chain.role: Archbishop of Constantinople, Doctor of the Church
chain.connection: Fellow student with Basil the Great; friend of Athanasius

chain.name: Apostolic tradition through the Cappadocian Fathers and Alexandrian school
chain.connection: Traceable through Origen, Clement, Pantaenus to Saint Mark and Saint Peter

chain.name: Saint Gregory the Great
chain.role: Pope, Bishop of Rome, Doctor of the Church
chain.connection: Bishop of Rome in unbroken succession from Saint Peter

chain.name: Apostolic See of Rome
chain.role: Unbroken succession of bishops from Saint Peter
chain.connection: Founded by the Apostles Peter and Paul

chain.name: Saint John Damascene
chain.role: Monk, Priest, Doctor of the Church
chain.connection: Trained by Cosmas the Monk, a Sicilian scholar; monk at the Monastery of Mar Saba near Jerusalem

chain.name: Monastery of Mar Saba
chain.connection: Ancient monastery in the Judean desert, in the apostolic tradition of Jerusalem

chain.name: Apostolic See of Jerusalem
chain.connection: Founded at Pentecost, first led by Saint James the Just

chain.name: Saint Vincent of Lerins
chain.connection: Monk at the Monastery of Lerins, Gaul; trained in the Gallic apostolic tradition

chain.name: Monastery of Lerins
chain.connection: Founded by Saint Honoratus; a center of orthodox teaching in the Western apostolic tradition

chain.name: Western Apostolic Tradition
chain.connection: The faith brought to Gaul by missionaries from Rome and the East, including Saint Irenaeus

chain.name: Saint Leo the Great
chain.role: Pope, Bishop of Rome, Doctor of the Church
chain.connection: Bishop of Rome in unbroken succession from Saint Peter

chain.name: Apostolic See of Rome
chain.role: Unbroken succession of bishops from Saint Peter
chain.connection: Founded by the Apostles Peter and Paul

chain.name: Saint Gregory of Nyssa
chain.connection: Brother of Saint Basil the Great; trained by Basil and their sister Saint Macrina the Younger

chain.name: Saint Basil the Great
chain.connection: Grandson of Saint Macrina the Elder, disciple of Gregory Thaumaturgus

chain.name: Saint Gregory Thaumaturgus
chain.connection: Pupil of Origen in Caesarea

chain.name: Origen
chain.role: Head of the Catechetical School of Alexandria
chain.connection: In the apostolic tradition through Clement, Pantaenus, and Saint Mark

chain.name: Theodore the Studite
chain.role: Abbot of the Stoudios Monastery, Constantinople
chain.connection: Monk and abbot in the tradition of the Church of Constantinople

chain.name: Church of Constantinople
chain.connection: Tradition traces the founding of the church to the Apostle Andrew

chain.name: Second Council of Nicaea
chain.connection: Ecumenical council of bishops in apostolic succession from all major sees

chain.name: The Apostolic Sees
chain.connection: Founded by or tracing authority to the original apostles

chain.name: Council of Ephesus
chain.connection: Council of bishops in apostolic succession, convened at Ephesus — the city where Saint John and Saint Paul taught

chain.name: Church of Ephesus
chain.connection: Founded by Saint Paul; later led by the Apostle John and his disciple Saint Polycarp

chain.name: Council of Carthage
chain.role: Regional council that ratified the biblical canon
chain.connection: Council of North African bishops in communion with Rome

chain.name: Church of Carthage
chain.connection: In communion with and under the authority of the apostolic See of Rome

chain.name: Pope Damasus I
chain.connection: Bishop of Rome in unbroken succession from Saint Peter

chain.name: Apostolic See of Rome
chain.role: Unbroken succession of bishops from Saint Peter
chain.connection: Founded by the Apostles Peter and Paul

chain.name: Muratorian Fragment
chain.role: Earliest known list of New Testament books
chain.connection: Written by an unknown author in Rome, reflecting the canon received by the Roman church from the apostles

chain.name: Church of Rome
chain.role: Founded by Saints Peter and Paul
chain.connection: Founded by the Apostles Peter and Paul

chain.name: Catacomb Inscriptions
chain.role: Funerary inscriptions by early Roman Christians
chain.connection: Written by members of the Roman Christian community

chain.name: Church of Rome
chain.connection: Founded by the Apostles Peter and Paul

chain.name: Sub Tuum Praesidium
chain.role: Oldest known prayer to the Blessed Virgin Mary
chain.connection: Found on a papyrus from Egypt (Rylands Papyrus 470), reflecting the devotion of the Egyptian Christian community

chain.name: Church of Alexandria / Egypt
chain.connection: Saint Mark the Evangelist, companion of Saint Peter

chain.name: Leontius of Neapolis
chain.connection: Bishop in the Church of Cyprus

chain.name: Church of Cyprus
chain.connection: Evangelized by Saints Paul and Barnabas (Acts 13:4–12); Barnabas was a native of Cyprus

chain.name: Eusebius of Caesarea
chain.role: Bishop of Caesarea, Father of Church History
chain.connection: Student of Pamphilus, who studied in the library and school of Origen at Caesarea

chain.name: Pamphilus of Caesarea
chain.connection: Custodian of Origen's library; defender of Origen's legacy

chain.name: Origen
chain.role: Head of the Catechetical School of Alexandria, later Caesarea
chain.connection: In the apostolic tradition through Clement, Pantaenus, and Saint Mark

chain.name: Theodore of Mopsuestia
chain.connection: Studied alongside John Chrysostom under Diodore of Tarsus at the school of Antioch

chain.name: Diodore of Tarsus
chain.role: Bishop of Tarsus, Head of the School of Antioch
chain.connection: Trained in the Antiochene apostolic tradition

chain.name: Apostolic See of Antioch
chain.role: Church where believers were first called Christians
chain.connection: Founded by the Apostle Peter
